text: Git branching is an essential part of using version control software like Git. A common workflow involves creating feature branches, which are separate copies of the main code. Developers can work on new features or bug fixes in these branches without affecting the stable codebase. Once the work is complete, a pull request is often created to propose the changes. This allows team members to review the code, discuss improvements, and catch errors before the changes are integrated. Finally, the modified branch is merged back into the main branch, combining the updates. This process keeps the project organised, reduces conflicts, and helps teams collaborate effectively on software development.
